response:
In C# it is possible to estimate the time taken for a UDP packet to reach a server  
but it is not guaranteed to be accurate  
UDP does not provide built in mechanisms for measuring round trip time  
Unlike TCP which includes acknowledgment  
To measure one way delay you need to implement a custom solution  
You can send a packet and record the time  
Then receive the response and calculate the difference  
This requires the server to respond to the packet  
The server must be configured to reply to incoming UDP packets  
You can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient class  
Send the packet and record the timestamp  
Wait for the response and calculate the time difference  
This method is not reliable due to network conditions  
It may take some time for the packet to reach the server  
You can use the Send and Receive methods of UdpClient  
This approach can give an approximate measurement  
It is not the same as ping which measures round trip time  
You can use the DateTime class to track the time  
This method is not always accurate but can be useful  
It depends on the network and server configuration
